title: Black Box Data After a Semi Crash in Augusta, Georgia
twitter: Semi crash in Augusta, GA? The truck's black box data can vanish in 30 days. Preserve your evidence NOW. Call 855-WRECKMATCH (855-897-3256) or visit https://www.wreckmatch.com/blog/black-box-data-after-a-semi-crash-in-augusta-georgia — Georgia's 2-year clock is already ticking. ⚠️
linkedin:
If your company handles trucking litigation or represents injury victims in Georgia, black box data preservation is one of the most time-sensitive issues in any semi-truck crash case — and most clients don't know it until it's too late.

After a semi crash in Augusta, Georgia, the truck's Electronic Control Module (ECM) captures critical data: speed at impact, brake application, throttle position, and driver hours. Federal regulations require carriers to retain certain records, but that doesn't mean the data survives automatically. Without a formal legal hold or spoliation letter sent within days of the crash, that evidence can be overwritten, lost, or destroyed.

WreckMatch connects injured victims with attorneys from our 800+ law firm network who understand the urgency of preserving black box evidence and navigating Georgia's trucking laws. Speed matters here — not just in settlement, but in evidence collection.

If you or someone you know was injured in a semi-truck collision in the Augusta area, don't wait. The 2-year statute of limitations sounds like plenty of time, but the black box data window closes far sooner.

Connect with a qualified truck accident attorney today: https://www.wreckmatch.com/#form
facebook:
If you or a loved one was hit by a semi-truck in Augusta, Georgia, one of the most important pieces of evidence in your case is already counting down — the truck's black box.

Most people don't realize that black box data from commercial trucks can be overwritten or lost within 30 days if no one takes legal action to preserve it. This data can show exactly how fast the truck was going, whether the brakes were applied, and how many hours the driver had been on the road — all critical facts that could make or break your case.

Georgia gives you 2 years to file a claim, but that doesn't mean you have 2 years to act on the evidence. A spoliation letter needs to go out fast, and that requires an experienced truck accident attorney moving quickly on your behalf.

Don't let the most important evidence in your case disappear before you even know you need it. Have you or someone you know ever dealt with evidence being lost after a truck accident?
reddit_title: Semi truck hit me in Augusta, GA — what is a black box and does it actually help my case?
reddit_body:
If you were recently in a crash involving a semi-truck in Augusta, Georgia, you may have heard someone mention the truck's 'black box.' This is actually the truck's Electronic Control Module (ECM) or Event Data Recorder (EDR), and it captures data like vehicle speed, brake application, throttle position, and engine performance in the seconds before impact. In serious crashes, this data can be the difference between proving fault and having no evidence at all.

The critical thing to understand is that this data does not automatically get preserved. Commercial carriers are required to keep certain logs under federal FMCSA regulations, but ECM data can be overwritten during normal truck operation — sometimes within 30 days. To lock the data down, an attorney typically sends a spoliation letter demanding the carrier preserve all electronic records. The sooner this happens after a crash, the better your chances of having that evidence available.

Georgia has a 2-year statute of limitations for personal injury cases, but the black box window is much shorter. If you are trying to figure out your next steps, it helps to talk to an attorney who specifically handles truck accident cases and knows how to move fast on evidence. Sites like wreckmatch.com connect crash victims with truck accident attorneys in their area — worth checking out if you are not sure where to start.
